preamble: The shift from the bilabial nasal [m] to the velar nasal [ŋ] (ng) is a process of regressive place assimilation. It occurs when a bilabial nasal anticipates a following velar consonant (like [k] or [ɡ]), causing the place of articulation to move from the lips back to the soft palate.

examples.note: Rapid speech assimilation of the bilabial nasal before the velar stop [ɡ].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
